lesson.subtitle: Aula 13 -- Presenting the Numbers -- Reporting Results
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rafael de Andrade Brandão | Aula 13 -- Presenting the Numbers

listenings.text: Hi Rafael, this is Diana from the leadership team. I have great news. After we closed the deal last week, the board wants to hear about your results. We scheduled a short presentation for Friday morning. I want you to present the sales numbers from the last quarter to the team. Please prepare three or four slides with a chart. Start with the highlights, then explain what increased and what decreased, and finish with your plan for the next quarter. The audience is small, only eight people, so keep it simple and clear. Remember to use the past simple, because you are reporting what already happened. I know you prepared a strong report, and I am sure the presentation is going to be excellent. Good luck, and send me the slides before Friday.

Good morning, everyone, and thank you for coming. Today I am going to present the sales results from the last quarter. I prepared four slides with a chart, so let us start. First, the highlights. Last quarter we signed three new contracts, and we closed the biggest deal of the year. Then, the numbers. Our sales increased by fifteen percent, and our costs decreased by five percent. After that, I want to show you the chart on the next slide. As you can see, the growth was strong in the last three months. Finally, my plan. Next quarter, I am going to focus on two new markets. That is the end of my presentation. Thank you for listening. Do you have any questions for me?

extra_audio.text: Diana invites Rafael to present the quarterly results on Friday. Rafael prepares four slides with a chart. He starts with the highlights and the new contracts. Then he explains that sales increased and costs decreased. Finally, he shows the chart and presents his plan for the next quarter.
